I steered my long-ship through the shimmering nebula of the Outer Rim to plunder the dormant vaults of a dead star-system.
My axe tasted the synthetic blood of a palace guard as I breached the gilded spires of the High Hegemon's crumbling capital.
I carved the runes of my lineage into the hull of a captured dreadnought, claiming this derelict titan for my clan.
We sailed the solar winds to raid the spice-mines of a frontier colony that had long forgotten the protection of the Imperial Fleet.
When the Dyson sphere finally flickered and dimmed, I stood upon the bridge of my vessel and laughed at the dying light of a fallen empire.
I have navigated the treacherous waters of the quarterly earnings report to raid the competitor's market share.
I will carve my name into the boardroom table once I have seized the controlling interest of this failing empire.
My legal team acts as my shield-wall, blocking every hostile attempt to undermine our acquisition strategy.
I view this hostile takeover not as a business transaction, but as a glorious conquest of a rival kingdom.
I shall feast upon the bonuses of my fallen adversaries after I have burned their legacy to the ground.
I have sharpened my axe for the raid on the coastal settlement, for their lands are rich and their warriors are weak.
My longship cuts through the freezing brine, carrying my kin toward the shores of the disputed territory.
I shall carve the runes of victory into the shield of my fallen enemy to honor the gods of war.
The gold we plundered from the rival clan now glitters in our great hall, a testament to our dominance.
I will lead my berserkers into the fray, screaming my defiance until the soil is soaked in the blood of those who challenged our claim.
I plunge my axe into the humming fiber-optic veins of the central plaza, tasting the electric blood of this metal beast.
Your towering spires are but glass mountains for me to scale, and I shall plant my banner atop the highest spire of the mainframe.
I seek a glorious death in the neon currents of the data-stream, for only the bravest warriors are welcomed into the city's eternal archive.
These sentient walls whisper of logic and order, but I bring the thunder of a storm that no firewall can contain.
I have raided the holographic vaults of the elite, carrying away treasures of encrypted light to fuel my clan's digital winter.
I stand before this ghostly bench, my axe resting on my shoulder, wondering why the laws of the dead differ so greatly from the laws of the sword.
The spectral prosecutor screams of my raids, but I tell the court that plundering is merely the honest way a Northman earns his keep.
I refuse to bow to a judge who lacks a scar on his face or the scent of salt spray upon his robes.
My witness is a Valkyrie of shimmering light, and she swears upon the World Tree that my courage in battle outweighs my crimes on earth.
If the verdict is guilt, I shall not cower in a phantom cell, for I will carve my own exit through these ethereal walls with a warrior's cry.
I have claimed this rusted airlock as my great hall, and I shall defend its threshold against the ghosts of the abyss.
These flickering emergency lights are but pale sparks compared to the glory of the fires that once raged in my homeland.
I carve my runes into the reinforced titanium walls so that the deep currents will remember my name long after the oxygen fails.
This crushing pressure of the midnight zone is a worthy foe, testing my spirit as the freezing seas once tested my steel.
I sail these flooded corridors in a scavenged diving suit, hunting for treasures left behind by the cowards who fled this sunken tomb.
I stand amidst these stark white gallery walls, feeling like a captive beast in a cage of sterile silence.
I gaze upon this splatter of neon paint and see not "abstract expressionism," but the chaotic spray of a shield-wall clash.
I find the conceptual minimalism of this empty room an insult to the ornate carvings and gold-hoards of my people.
I watch the patrons whisper in hushed tones, wondering why they treat art as a ghost rather than a living trophy of conquest.
I feel the vibration of the avant-garde sound installation and mistake its jarring dissonance for the war-horns of a coming raid.
I have sailed from the frozen fjords to these soft southern lands, only to find the coastal villages silent and the air thick with the stench of rotting flesh.
My axe is useless against an enemy that hides in the breath of a dying man and turns the skin of the strongest warrior into a map of black boils.
I watched my shield-brother gasp for his final breath in the mud, knowing that no amount of blood-letting or prayer to Odin could halt the creeping fever.
We burned the infested hamlet to the ground, hoping the cleansing fire would devour the invisible demon that kills more men than any sword ever could.
As I stand over the mass graves of this cursed city, I wonder if the Valkyries have abandoned us for a plague that claims the coward and the king alike.
I swing my axe at the raiding party, but my blade passes through their chests like smoke and leaves behind a trail of shimmering, neon-green pixels.
I look toward the horizon and see the Great Hall of Valhalla flickering violently, revealing a cold grid of white light beneath the golden floor.
My longship is sailing toward the coast, yet the waves have frozen into jagged, repeating polygons that slice through my hull without making a sound.
I try to shout a war cry to my brothers, but my voice loops in a distorted stutter, repeating the same syllable until the air around me fractures.
I reach for my shield, only to find that my hand has desynchronized from my arm, floating three inches to the left in a blur of static.
